root_text.sn55.20:1.1: Atha kho bhagavā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—jetavane antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
root_text.sn55.20:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā tāvatiṁsakāyikā devatāyo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā devatāyo bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.20:2.1: “Sādhu kho, āvuso, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn55.20:2.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.20:2.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, āvuso, evam’idhekacce sattā sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā.
root_text.sn55.20:2.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.20:2.7: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, āvuso, evam’idhekacce sattā sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā”ti.
root_text.sn55.20:3.1: “Sādhu kho, mārisa, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn55.20:3.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.20:3.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa, evamayaṁ pajā sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā.
root_text.sn55.20:3.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.20:3.7: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa, evamayaṁ pajā sotāpannā avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā”ti.

translation_text.sn55.20:1.1: Then the Buddha, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared among the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn55.20:1.2: Then several deities of the company of the thirty-three went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side. The Buddha said to them:
translation_text.sn55.20:2.1: “Reverends, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha. …
translation_text.sn55.20:2.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings are stream-enterers, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.20:2.4: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching. …
translation_text.sn55.20:2.6: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.20:2.7: It’s the reason why some sentient beings are stream-enterers, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
translation_text.sn55.20:3.1: “Good fellow, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.20:3.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings are stream-enterers, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.20:3.4: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching. …
translation_text.sn55.20:3.6: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.20:3.7: It’s the reason why some sentient beings are stream-enterers, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
